topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The plastic cup felt slick in my trembling hand as I stared at the printout. The words, stark and undeniable, swam before my eyes: "Adoption Match: Biological Parent Identified." A hollow ache settled deep in my chest, a cold weight that made it hard to draw a full breath. I traced the names on the paper, each letter a tiny, sharp shard. My shoulders slumped, the tension in my neck radiating outward. I sank onto the edge of the bed, pulling my knees up to my chest, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole. The silence of the room amplified the frantic thumping in my ears.

[story 2]

I kept running my thumb over the DNA results, the fluorescent light of the computer screen reflecting in my wide, unblinking eyes. It wasn't a shock that hit me immediately; it was a slow, creeping numbness, like being submerged in thick, cold water. Every muscle in my body felt heavy, resistant to movement. I stood up slowly, the movement stiff, and walked to the window, looking out at the busy street below as if it were happening to someone else. A single tear escaped, tracing a hot, slow path down my cheek, quickly followed by a desperate swipe of the heel.

[story 3]

When the confirmation came, the world seemed to tilt on its axis. I sat at the kitchen table, the paper spread out like a map of uncharted territory. My hands shook so violently I had to grip the edge of the wood to steady them. A profound emptiness expanded in my core, leaving me feeling strangely light and utterly adrift. I didn't cry; instead, I just stared at the grain of the wood, my gaze unfocused, lost somewhere beyond the familiar walls of the room. A deep, weary sigh escaped my lips, a sound heavy with unspoken sorrow.
